qa_pairs.question: How can you verify that a Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injection text file has been successfully loaded in ANSYS Fluent's graphical user interface (GUI)?
qa_pairs.answer: If a Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injection text file is successfully loaded in ANSYS Fluent's GUI, no console errors or warning messages will appear. You will be returned to the "Injections" dialog box, where you should see the specified Injection name and be able to list its Particles and Properties in the console.

qa_pairs.question: In ANSYS Fluent, how do you enable the updating of Discrete Phase Model (DPM) source terms every flow iteration, and where can this setting be stored?
qa_pairs.answer: To enable updating Discrete Phase Model (DPM) source terms every flow iteration in ANSYS Fluent, you need to open the Discrete Phase Model Dialog Box and select "Interaction with Continuous Phase." This setting can be saved directly in your case (.cas) file or added through a journal file.

qa_pairs.question: After verifying a Discrete Phase Model (DPM) injection in the GUI, what commands can be added to an ANSYS Fluent journal file to automate its setup and enable interaction with the continuous phase?
qa_pairs.answer: To automate the DPM injection setup and enable interaction with the continuous phase via a journal file after solution initialization, you can add commands such as: `/define/models/dpm/interaction/coupled-calculations yes` to enable source term updates, followed by commands like `/define/models/dpm/injections/delete-injection injection-0:1` to remove existing injections, and then `/define/models/dpm/injections/create injection-0:1 no yes file no zinjection01.inj no no no no` to create a new file injection, and finally, `/define/models/dpm/injections/list-particles injection-0:1` and `/define/models/dpm/injections/list-injection-properties injection-0:1` to confirm the setup.
